message:
test(proptest/error_renderer): pin category serialization contract (follow-on to 1bb10288)

The error_renderer fix in 1bb10288 switched category JSON emission from
format!("{:?}", def.category) to serde_json::json!(def.category), so the
rendered "category" field now carries whatever the enum's Serialize impl
produces (typically a serde-attribute-renamed snake_case string, not
Debug-form CamelCase). This commit adds proptest coverage that pins the
contract at both renderer entry points so future refactors cannot
silently regress back to Debug-format.

## New property #10: JSON render category matches catalog

```rust
fn json_render_category_matches_catalog(error in arb_core_error()) {
    let code = ErrorRenderer::error_code(&error);
    let renderer = ErrorRenderer::new(OutputFormat::Json);
    let output = renderer.render(&error);
    let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
    if let Some(def) = get_error_code(code) {
        let expected = serde_json::to_value(def.category).unwrap();
        prop_assert_eq!(parsed["category"], expected, ...);
    }
}
```

80 cases per run, gated on arb_core_error() so every concrete Error
variant that currently renders to a code with a catalog entry gets
exercised. The `if let Some(def)` is defensive — any code without a
catalog entry is covered by property #19 (all error codes have catalog
entries) and would be flagged there, not here.

## Upgraded property #25 (previously "has category field")

The old #24 only asserted `parsed["category"].is_string()`. That
accepted BOTH the Debug-format "Configuration" and the serialized
"configuration" forms — so it silently passed the pre-1bb10288 bug.
Strengthen to an equality check against
serde_json::to_value(def.category) so the test fails fast if the
renderer ever drifts back.

Both properties build their expected value through the same
serialization path the code uses (serde_json::to_value(category)),
so the assertions stay correct even if a future ErrorCategory enum
gets its #[serde(...)] renames changed — the property checks that the
renderer's output MATCHES the Serialize impl, whatever that impl
currently produces.

## Header renumbering

Inserting the new property at position 10 shifts every subsequent
property number by 1 (10..35 -> 11..36). Module-level //! doc comment
index and per-block `// N. ...` separators both renumbered consistently.
No other bodies changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
